title: Post-Process and Cinematic Screenshots

video.note: Students build a castle environment in Unreal Engine while recording daily screen/camera evidence for a making-of documentary.

learningTarget: I can use post-process and cinematic framing to capture polished castle visuals for the final documentary.

bellRinger.prompt: What makes a screenshot feel cinematic instead of accidental?

vocabulary.definition: A dated record of what was built, what was captured, what changed, and what needs to happen next.
Video captured from the computer screen to show workflow, testing, or before/after changes.
Camera or screen-recorded evidence that shows how a project was made.
Supporting footage that helps explain a process, place, detail, or action.
The Q3 Unreal scene students build, refine, walk through, and document.

slides.title: Q3 Lesson 14 - Post-Process and Cinematic Screenshots

assignment.title: Cinematic Screenshot and Post-Process Pass
evidenceRequired: Two cinematic screenshots of the castle environment.
Daily documentary clip showing shot setup or post-process adjustment.

exitTicket: Which cinematic shot might belong in your final documentary?
